surface_verification.justification: Backend/CLI producer change in the shipwright-compliance plugin (SBOM license resolver + triage emitter + sbom.md generator). No web surface: the dedup-key contract consumed by the WebUI/audit_detector is unchanged, and sbom.md is a rendered doc. Verified empirically by the full compliance suite (638) + integration suite (141) + a real sbom.md regeneration against the worktree repo (diff confirmed: not-installed deps -> '-', 'Unknown Licenses' section removed, clean verdict).

behaviors.behavior: AC-1 detect_python_license: NOT_INSTALLED when no dist-info, UNKNOWN_LICENSE when dist-info present w/o license
AC-2 detect_npm_license: NOT_INSTALLED when absent, UNKNOWN_LICENSE when present-no-license incl blank SPDX object
AC-3 collector: NOT_INSTALLED never becomes a triage group (python+npm)
AC-4 genuine Fall-2 still drives per-workspace/cluster triage
AC-5 doc: not-installed rendered '-', excluded from unique licenses/pie/verdict
AC-6 doc: Fall-2 'Dependencies Without a Declared License' section; old 'Unknown Licenses' removed
behaviors.evidence: test_sbom_not_installed.py::test_genuine_no_license_surfaced_as_section PASSED; sbom.md regen diff confirmed section removed

behaviors.behavior: AC-8 no scan-coverage / install-nag line; doc ASCII-only
AC-9 repo's 4 open SBOM items auto-dismiss on next regen (keys leave current_keys)
behaviors.evidence: mechanism proven: TestCollectorSilence (not-installed -> no group) + existing test_auto_resolve_when_workspace_clean / cluster auto-resolve (key absent -> dismissed sbomResolved)
